audits.summary: Audit of the Whirlpools CLMM program found no critical issues; medium findings related to arithmetic edge cases and access-control checks were remediated before launch.

audits.scope: Whirlpools concentrated liquidity program (independent second audit)
audits.summary: Neodyme identified one high-severity rounding/precision issue in tick math that was fixed prior to mainnet deployment, plus several lower-severity findings all addressed.
Classic pool audit found no critical or high issues; one medium finding around pool initialization order was remediated.

exploit_history.description: No confirmed exploits or hacks of Orca Protocol appear in Rekt.news archives or DefiLlama hacks database as of mid-2025. The protocol has operated without a material security incident since mainnet launch in 2021.

dependencies.provider: Pyth Network (on-chain TWAP via Whirlpool tick data; Pyth used by integrators)
dependencies.notes: Orca's core AMM does not rely on an external price oracle for swaps (price is derived from pool state), but many integrators and liquidation protocols built on top use Pyth. A Pyth manipulation could affect downstream protocols, not Orca core directly.

Orca is entirely dependent on Solana validator liveness and consensus. Solana has experienced multiple network outages (2021-2022); a halt would freeze all Orca liquidity. Solana's track record has improved significantly since 2023.

dependencies.provider: Wormhole (for bridged assets traded on Orca)
dependencies.notes: Orca pools frequently contain Wormhole-wrapped assets (e.g., wETH, wBTC). Wormhole suffered a $320M exploit in Feb 2022. A future Wormhole compromise could render wrapped assets in Orca pools worthless, though Orca's own contracts would be unaffected.

rationale: Orca has a relatively strong security posture for a Solana DEX: two independent audits of its core Whirlpools program with no critical findings at launch, zero confirmed exploits in ~4 years of operation, and a $500K Immunefi bug bounty. The primary residual risks are systemic — dependence on Solana network stability and exposure to Wormhole-bridged asset risk in its liquidity pools — rather than weaknesses in Orca's own smart contracts.

_data_quality_notes.audit_finding_counts: PARTIAL — exact high/med/low counts are approximated from publicly referenced summaries; full PDFs at the GitHub audit paths require direct download verification. Counts marked should be treated as indicative, not authoritative.
_data_quality_notes.contributors_count: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — GitHub unauthenticated API does not return full contributor lists for repos with >30 contributors without pagination via token.
_data_quality_notes.weekly_commits_avg: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — requires GitHub API authentication or a paid analytics tool.
_data_quality_notes.multisig_details: PARTIAL — Orca has publicly referenced multisig governance but exact signer count/threshold not formally published in a verifiable on-chain dashboard as of research date; 5-of-3 figure is from community references and should be verified via Squads Protocol on Solana.
_data_quality_notes.bug_bounty_max: PARTIAL — Immunefi listing for Orca shows tiered bounty; $500K figure reflects the critical smart contract tier as publicly listed but may have changed.
